tech_name: Microsoft Tablet PC / Windows XP Tablet PC Edition
notes: Gates-championed pen-computing PC form factor launched Nov 2002; Windows XP Tablet PC Edition; never achieved predicted majority adoption; displaced by iPad (2010) and capacitive tablets
Stylus-input computing tradition from GO PenPoint (1991), Apple Newton, Palm Graffiti, Tablet PC; now ubiquitous in consumer tablets and 2-in-1 devices with active digitizers
Microsoft enterprise/server OS kernel line; became Windows 2000/XP/Server 2003 and all modern Windows
Consumer-plus-business NT-kernel Windows release; XP Home, Pro, Media Center, Tablet PC Edition; most widely-deployed Windows release; end-of-support 2014
